likely_functional_target: MFSD12 - (self; functionally validated melanosome pH/lysosome regulator)
pigmentation_traits: facial pigmentation (polar/UV light); skin pigmentation; cutaneous melanoma
eqtl_target_gene: CACTIN (weak, GTEx_fibroblast p=0.023; not dominant over self)
evidence_citation: PMID:29489754 (Crawford et al. 2017, Science) - MFSD12 knockdown/knockout in zebrafish and mouse alters pigmentation
notes: MFSD12 is a functionally validated causal pigmentation gene independent of eQTL evidence - correctly labeled, just absent from the 168-gene network. The weak CACTIN eQTL signal does not override direct functional evidence for MFSD12 itself.

evidence_citation: eQTL Catalogue query (this project, TwinsUK skin dataset QTD000544)
notes: PKHD1 is confirmed as its own eQTL target in skin (p=3e-14), dominant over all nearby genes queried. Correctly-labeled locus; PKHD1 itself is simply absent from the 168-gene network.

likely_functional_target: SLC24A4 - (self; established melanosomal cation exchanger, paralog of SLC24A5)
pigmentation_traits: hair color (blond vs brown/black); eye color; suntan
eqtl_target_gene: SLC24A4 self not significant in queried bulk datasets; ATXN3 secondary hit weak/not dominant
evidence_citation: Sulem et al. 2007, Nat Genet 39:1443 (established GWAS hair/eye-colour gene)
notes: SLC24A4 is an established, functionally-plausible causal pigmentation gene; no significant bulk-tissue eQTL redirect to a different network gene was found. Correctly-labeled; simply absent from the 168-gene network.

pigmentation_traits: hair color (strand of hair color); eye color
eqtl_dataset_pvalue: GTEx_fibroblast QTD000216 p<1e-300; GTEx_skin_suprapubic QTD000311 p<1e-300 (dominant, self, across all 5 datasets)
notes: TSPAN10 is co-mapped with NPLOC4 by the GWAS Catalog pipeline at this locus. Our eQTL query confirms TSPAN10 itself as the dominant target gene (self-eQTL p<1e-300 across every skin/fibroblast dataset). TSPAN10 is the better-supported causal gene at this locus relative to NPLOC4, but TSPAN10 itself remains outside the 168-gene network.

likely_functional_target: TSPAN10 - (dominant skin/fibroblast eQTL target, co-mapped locus)
pigmentation_traits: hair color (strand of hair color); eye color
eqtl_dataset_pvalue: GTEx_fibroblast QTD000216 p<1e-300; GTEx_skin_suprapubic QTD000311 p<1e-300; consistent across TwinsUK/GENCORD
evidence_citation: eQTL Catalogue query (this project, GTEx v8 + TwinsUK + GENCORD skin/fibroblast datasets)
notes: NPLOC4 and TSPAN10 share the same GWAS-mapped variants at this locus. Our eQTL query shows TSPAN10 as the dominant, highly significant target across every skin/fibroblast dataset queried, while NPLOC4 itself does not appear among top hits. TSPAN10 is itself dark-matter (absent from network), so this locus does not collapse into an EXISTING network node, but TSPAN10 is the better-supported functional target relative to NPLOC4.

likely_functional_target: LINC01679 - (non-coding RNA; dominant eQTL target, not eligible for the protein-coding 168-gene network)
pigmentation_traits: hair color (strand of hair color); eye color
eqtl_dataset_pvalue: TwinsUK_skin QTD000544 p=1.17e-10; GTEx_fibroblast QTD000216 p=1.95e-05 (dominant over SIK1 self, best p=1.3e-3)
evidence_citation: eQTL Catalogue query (this project, TwinsUK skin + GTEx fibroblast)
notes: SIK1's top pigmentation variant shows a dominant eQTL effect on the adjacent long non-coding RNA LINC01679, not on SIK1 itself (SIK1 self-eQTL ~2 orders of magnitude weaker). LINC01679 is non-coding and outside the scope of the protein-coding 168-gene network, so this locus does not collapse into an existing network node, but SIK1 is unlikely to be the functional target.

eqtl_target_gene: UMPS (top hit, weak, GTEx_fibroblast); KALRN self does NOT appear among eQTL Catalogue rows for this rsid in any queried dataset

evidence_citation: PMID:37294081 (Chen et al., single population-specific GWAS); Ang2023_eLife (independent low-MAF replication + explicit caution)
notes: KALRN does carry a genuine pigmentation-trait GWAS hit (unlike ATRN/EMCN/MANBA/SLC39A8/TACR3), but at MAF=0.017 in a single population and independently flagged as unreliable in the case cohort. CORRECTION: an earlier draft of this row incorrectly attributed a 'KALRN self-eQTL p=0.010 (TwinsUK)' value to rs676091; re-verification of handoff/eqtl_results.json shows that self-eQTL value actually belongs to a DIFFERENT KALRN variant, rs2332719 (TwinsUK_skin QTD000544, p=0.0100798) - and rs2332719 is not itself a validated pigmentation variant (its GWAS hit is for educational attainment, not pigmentation, so it was correctly excluded from this table). rs676091 itself has zero KALRN self-eQTL rows in any queried dataset; its only eQTL hits are weak, non-dominant UMPS/OSBPL11 associations. Insufficient evidence for a network redirect.
